title: Vivarium Population Spenser: Internal migration module

materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"dqro7","text":"The tables used to choose the pool of individuals that are going to internally migrate are found here:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bue0e","text":"InternalOutmig2011_LEEDS2.csv","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"UNDERLINE","offset":0,"length":29}],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":29}],"data":[]},{"key":"8iree","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c4oig","text":"The age-gender MSOA migration matrices can be found in here:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"UNDERLINE","offset":0,"length":60}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"47f93","text":"https://github.com/alan-turing-institute/daedalus/tree/develop/persistent_data/od_matrices","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":0,"length":90}],"data":[]},{"key":"60ulc","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"A28B17AE1AA411EB96E00A58A9FEAC2A","url":"https://github.com/alan-turing-institute/daedalus/blob/develop/persistent_data/InternalOutmig2011_LEEDS2.csv"}},"1":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"C4A7BCF81AA411EB96E00A58A9FEAC2A","url":"https://github.com/alan-turing-institute/daedalus/tree/develop/persistent_data/od_matrices"}}}}
steps: This module models the [internal_migration](src/vivarium_population_spenser/internal_migration.py) between MSOAs (and their respective LADs) of individuals based on their gender, age, initial location (local authority level) and ethnicity. The input table to establish the pool of migrants that internally migrate based on their characteristics is [InternalOutmig2011_LEEDS2.csv](persistant_data/InternalOutmig2011_LEEDS2.csv). Once the pool of migrants is chosen they are assigned to a new MSOA based on their age and gender using the MSOA migration matrices in [od_matrices](persistant_data/od_matrices).
Divide the annual outmigration rates for that local authority by the number of time steps existing in a year.
Select all individuals in the sample that appear as "alive", have an associated gender and haven't internally migrated in the las 365 days.
For these individuals, get the internal outmigration rate given their age, gender, ethnicity and location and turn it into a probability.
Using random sampling and the internal outmigration probability of the individuals, choose which individuals are going to internally migrate in that time step.
From this pool of individuals use an age and gender specific MSOA to MSOA migration matrix to choose which MSOA (and their respective local authority) they are going to migrate to.
Update their information in the state table with their new location and the time they internally migrated.
creator: Camila Rangel Smith
